code_summary_rows.label: Access to Information and Records
code_summary_rows.definition: Access denied or delayed to information or records.

code_summary_rows.label: Language and Communication Barriers
code_summary_rows.definition: Information not provided in understandable language or format; no interpreter provided.

code_summary_rows.label: Willful Interference with Ombudsman Program
code_summary_rows.definition: Intentional prevention of or interference with resident or complainant accessing the ombudsman program.
code_summary_rows.notes: No direct facility F-tag when interference is by someone other than the facility.

code_summary_rows.label: Admission
code_summary_rows.definition: Admitted against wishes; admission agreement missing or has illegal provisions.

code_summary_rows.label: Appeal Process
code_summary_rows.definition: Resident did not receive information on appeal rights or assistance filing; facility failed to follow appeal ruling.

code_summary_rows.label: Room Issues
code_summary_rows.definition: Request for room change denied; objection to planned room change; inadequate notice of change.

code_summary_rows.label: Live in Less Restrictive Setting
code_summary_rows.definition: Not offered transition planning; request to return to community denied, ignored, or inadequately addressed.

code_summary_rows.label: Resident or Family Council
code_summary_rows.definition: Interference with formation; staff doesn't assist or exerts too much control; doesn't respond to council requests.

code_summary_rows.label: Billing and Charges
code_summary_rows.definition: Incorrect/improper billing; overcharging; raising rates without notice; deposits not refunded; billing for services not provided.
code_summary_rows.notes: No direct F-tag covers billing disputes; F567-F570 cover trust fund management, not billing errors per se.

code_summary_rows.label: Personal Property
code_summary_rows.definition: Loss or mismanagement by facility of resident property including money or trust fund.
code_summary_rows.notes: F602 only applies when facility or facility staff misappropriate; if family member, refer to APS.

code_summary_rows.label: Accidents and Falls
code_summary_rows.definition: Unexpected or unintended incident or injury of unknown origin; may result in injury or illness.

code_summary_rows.label: Personal Hygiene
code_summary_rows.definition: Failure to provide hygiene services; not bathed timely; allowed to remain in soiled clothing/incontinent briefs; hands/face not washed; teeth/dentures not cleaned.

code_summary_rows.label: Access to Health-Related Services
code_summary_rows.definition: Failure to obtain/maintain healthcare services based on change in condition, resident request, or not obtained timely.

code_summary_rows.label: Symptoms Unattended
code_summary_rows.definition: Failure to accommodate, identify, or provide services related to change in condition; symptoms not addressed and care not provided.

code_summary_rows.label: Incontinence Care
code_summary_rows.definition: Failure to provide incontinence/catheter care timely as requested or per care plan; not providing necessary bowel/bladder training.

code_summary_rows.label: Assistive Devices or Equipment
code_summary_rows.definition: Failure to provide access to assistive devices/supports; failure to maintain adaptive equipment.

code_summary_rows.label: Rehabilitation Services
code_summary_rows.definition: Failure to provide/arrange rehabilitation therapies; resident not assisted/encouraged to improve or maintain function or ambulate.

code_summary_rows.label: Physical Restraint
code_summary_rows.definition: Use of device attached to body that cannot be removed by resident, deliberately preventing free movement or normal access to body.

code_summary_rows.label: Chemical Restraint
code_summary_rows.definition: Use of any drug for discipline or convenience not required to treat medical symptoms.

code_summary_rows.label: Infection Control
code_summary_rows.definition: Insufficient measures to prevent or control infection.

code_summary_rows.label: Activities
code_summary_rows.definition: Lack of choice and appropriate activities; facility fails to consider individual preferences; variety limited or not offered; posted activities not conducted.

code_summary_rows.label: Transportation
code_summary_rows.definition: Failure to assist resident in obtaining transportation for community participation or healthcare appointments.
code_summary_rows.notes: No specific transportation F-tag exists; transportation is generally a quality-of-life issue under §483.10(f)(1).

code_summary_rows.label: Conflict Resolution
code_summary_rows.definition: Disagreement between residents where ombudsman assistance requested to achieve resolution.
code_summary_rows.notes: Conflict resolution between residents has no direct F-tag; ombudsman mediates under program authority.

code_summary_rows.label: Social Services
code_summary_rows.definition: Not available or appropriate; facility fails to provide social services to help resident achieve goals or access supports.

code_summary_rows.label: Therapeutic or Special Diet
code_summary_rows.definition: Therapeutic diet not served per care plan; special request diet or food preferences not accommodated.

code_summary_rows.label: Environment
code_summary_rows.definition: Building environment including safety hazards; room or water temperature too hot/cold; ventilation inadequate.

code_summary_rows.label: Building Structure
code_summary_rows.definition: Building interior/exterior not maintained; building hazards; poor lighting; building not secure.

code_summary_rows.label: Supplies, Storage and Furnishings
code_summary_rows.definition: Furnishings lacking or in disrepair; inadequate locked storage; shortage of supplies (bed linens, towels, toilet paper).

code_summary_rows.label: Accessibility
code_summary_rows.definition: Building and grounds accessibility complaints.
code_summary_rows.notes: No single dedicated ADA/accessibility F-tag in nursing home survey; accessibility is generally within F920-F926 range.

code_summary_rows.label: Housekeeping, Laundry and Pest Abatement
code_summary_rows.definition: Housekeeping inadequate/absent; rooms not clean; linens not changed; laundry not washed; pest control problems.

code_summary_rows.label: Administrative Oversight
code_summary_rows.definition: Mismanagement; administrator absent/unresponsive/inadequately trained/not supervising; incomplete/missing/falsified records; background screening not performed; illegal policies/practices.
code_summary_rows.notes: No single 'administrator absent' F-tag; cite §483.70 generally.

code_summary_rows.label: Fiscal Management
code_summary_rows.definition: Facility lacks fiscal resources resulting in staff shortages, staff alerts of not being paid, lack of food or supplies, utilities cut off.
code_summary_rows.notes: No direct 'fiscal crisis' F-tag; investigative tool is to cite the resulting care failures (F725, F801, etc.).

code_summary_rows.label: Regulatory System
code_summary_rows.definition: Complaints about actions or inactions of licensing/certification agency or similar regulatory agency.
code_summary_rows.notes: Outside agency issue; no facility F-tag applicable. Route to appropriate regulatory agency.

code_summary_rows.label: Medicaid
code_summary_rows.definition: Complaints regarding Medicaid eligibility, coverage, or issues related to Medicaid program.
code_summary_rows.notes: Outside agency issue; no facility F-tag applicable.

code_summary_rows.label: Managed Care
code_summary_rows.definition: Complaints regarding covered services, denial of services, or other managed care plan issues.
code_summary_rows.notes: Outside agency issue; no facility F-tag applicable.

code_summary_rows.label: Medicare
code_summary_rows.definition: Complaints regarding Medicare eligibility, coverage, or issues related to Medicare program.
code_summary_rows.notes: Outside agency issue; no facility F-tag applicable.

code_summary_rows.label: Veterans Affairs
code_summary_rows.definition: Complaints about eligibility, coverage, or issues related to DVA.
code_summary_rows.notes: Outside agency issue; no facility F-tag applicable.

code_summary_rows.label: Private Insurance
code_summary_rows.definition: Complaints regarding any private insurance company, covered services, or denial of payment.
code_summary_rows.notes: Outside agency issue; no facility F-tag applicable.

code_summary_rows.label: Resident Representative or Family Conflict
code_summary_rows.definition: Representative/family interferes with resident's decision making and preferences related to health, welfare, safety, or rights but does not rise to abuse/neglect/exploitation level.
code_summary_rows.notes: No direct F-tag; if representative conduct rises to exploitation, use A04/F602.

code_summary_rows.label: Services from Outside Provider
code_summary_rows.definition: Problems with services provided by individual or entity not associated with or arranged by the facility.
code_summary_rows.notes: No facility F-tag; outside provider complaints go to the relevant licensing authority.

code_summary_rows.label: Request to Transition to Community Setting
code_summary_rows.definition: Barriers to transition to community; inadequate assistance with housing, services and supports not related to facility action/inaction.
code_summary_rows.notes: Community transition barriers NOT caused by facility have no F-tag; barriers caused by facility inaction may implicate §483.15(b).

appendix_pp_page_rows.notes: No CFR section; no citations should ever be made under this tag.

Right to refuse transfer to another room

appendix_pp_page_rows.heading: Psychotropic drugs
appendix_pp_page_rows.notes: Present in Rev. 225 (page 566); deleted in Rev. 229. Use F605 for psychotropic/chemical restraint going forward.

appendix_pp_page_rows.heading: Medication Errors — free from medication error
Medication Errors — free from significant medication error

do_not_map_catalog.reason: F602 covers §483.12 Misappropriation of Resident Property/Exploitation (financial/property), not physical abuse. A01 (physical abuse) should not use F602 as a direct mapping.
do_not_map_catalog.correct_mapping_for_ftag: F602 maps to A04 (financial exploitation) as direct; E02 (personal property loss) as direct.

do_not_map_catalog.reason: B03 (willful interference with ombudsman program) has no direct facility F-tag when the interference is by someone other than the facility. Route to 45 CFR §1324.11(a)(1)(i) ombudsman access rights and 42 CFR §483.10(k)/F586 only when facility is the actor.

No direct F-tag covers billing disputes. F567-F570 cover resident trust fund management, not billing errors per se. Billing complaints are handled under facility contract/admission agreement obligations.

F602 only applies when the facility or facility staff misappropriate resident property. If a family member or other non-facility person is the actor, refer to Adult Protective Services. F602 must not be cited in E02 cases involving non-facility actors.
correct_ftags_for_nors_code: F602 (direct — when facility is actor only)

do_not_map_catalog.reason: No specific transportation F-tag exists in Appendix PP. Transportation complaints are a quality-of-life issue. The tool should suggest contacting the facility for transportation assistance under §483.10(f)(1) self-determination, not cite a survey tag.
Conflict resolution between residents is a pure ombudsman mediation role. No survey F-tag is applicable. Ombudsman acts under 45 CFR §1324.19(a)(1) program authority.

K01-K06 are outside agency issues. Appendix PP does not apply unless the facility separately committed a violation. Always route to the appropriate outside agency plus 45 CFR 1324.19/1324.13 ombudsman authority.
K02 Medicaid is an outside agency issue. No facility F-tag applicable unless facility separately committed a violation.
K03 Managed Care is an outside agency issue. No facility F-tag applicable unless facility separately committed a violation.
K04 Medicare is an outside agency issue. No facility F-tag applicable unless facility separately committed a violation.
K05 Veterans Affairs is an outside agency issue. No facility F-tag applicable unless facility separately committed a violation.
K06 Private Insurance is an outside agency issue. No facility F-tag applicable unless facility separately committed a violation.
L01 has no direct F-tag. If representative conduct rises to exploitation level, use A04/F602 instead. Ombudsman acts under 45 CFR §1324.19(b)(5)-(7) representative conflict procedures.
L02 has no facility F-tag. Outside provider complaints go to the relevant licensing authority. Ombudsman acts under 45 CFR §1324.19(a)(1) and §1324.13(h) MOU authority.

L03 community transition barriers NOT caused by facility have no F-tag. Barriers caused by facility inaction may implicate §483.15(b)/F561. Always distinguish between facility-caused and non-facility-caused barriers.

No single dedicated ADA/accessibility F-tag exists in the nursing home survey. Accessibility complaints fall generally within F920-F926 range under physical environment. Do not cite a single accessibility-specific tag.
No single 'administrator absent' F-tag exists. Cite §483.70 Administration generally; resulting care failures should be cited through the applicable care F-tags.
No direct 'fiscal crisis' F-tag exists. Investigative approach is to cite the resulting care failures (F725 insufficient nursing staff, F801 food and nutrition services, etc.).
correct_ftags_for_nors_code: F800/F801 series (related — food/nutrition if supplies lacking)

human_review_flags.description: F556 is a RESERVED tag. No citations should ever be made under this tag. Any tool logic that might auto-suggest F556 must be blocked.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Add hard block in tool logic: F556 = never suggest, never cite.

human_review_flags.description: F744 heading was not cleanly extracted from PDF. Likely §483.40(b) continuation. Exact heading is uncertain.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Human reviewer should manually verify F744 heading against current CMS Appendix PP PDF.

human_review_flags.description: G02 (Transportation) — no specific F-tag exists in Appendix PP. Tool should suggest contacting facility for transportation assistance under §483.10(f)(1) self-determination, NOT cite a survey tag.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Tool must display advisory: 'No transportation-specific F-tag exists. Suggest facility action under §483.10(f)(1).'

human_review_flags.description: G03 (Conflict Resolution) — pure ombudsman mediation role. No survey F-tag applicable. Ombudsman operates under 45 CFR §1324.19(a)(1).
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Tool must display advisory: 'Ombudsman mediates under program authority. No F-tag citation appropriate.'

human_review_flags.description: K01-K06 and L01-L03 codes must never trigger F-tag suggestions. Always route to appropriate outside agency plus 45 CFR 1324.19/1324.13 ombudsman authority.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Tool must enforce routing rule: K and L series → outside agency routing only, no Appendix PP F-tags.

human_review_flags.description: A01 + F602 mapping in existing crosswalk_catalog.json is INCORRECT. F602 covers financial misappropriation/exploitation (§483.12), not physical abuse. This mapping must be removed and corrected in the existing catalog.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: CODEX: Remove F602 from A01 mappings. Add F602 as direct mapping for A04 and E02. Verify crosswalk_catalog.json A01 entry uses only F600 (direct), F607 (related), F609 (related).

human_review_flags.description: F758 was present in Rev. 225 PDF (page 566) but was deleted in Rev. 229. Going forward, F605 must be used for psychotropic drug/chemical restraint complaints.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Tool must redirect any F758 reference to F605. Display notice: 'F758 was deleted in Rev. 229; use F605 for psychotropic/chemical restraint citations.'
